FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Trump's CFPB pick gains control over workforce cuts, Q2 bank earnings primed for strength on advisory fees and volatility, but rate-hike odds rising threatens NIM compression.

bullets.text: Brian Johnson, Trump's CFPB nominee, wins 60-day post-confirmation review period on staff-cut plan—gives him discretion over bureau workforce reduction.
July Fed rate-hike odds rising on Strait of Hormuz oil tensions; direct headwind for bank NIM compression and deposit-pricing dynamics going into earnings.
Big banks positioned for strong Q2 results: SpaceX IPO advisory fees, geopolitical volatility trading upside, and commercial lending pickup offset rate-hike concerns.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Russell Investments sold to CalPERS in major wealth management reshuffling; RIA consolidation accelerates across platform models amid leadership transitions.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments for $580B pension system's first multi-asset manager; Russell gains non-profit governance and debt relief via Vanguard-style playbook.
RIA consolidation heating up: Hightower hits organic growth highs, ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform, Choreo closes fourth deal post-SpaceX win.
Summitry ($4B RIA owned by Aspen PE) elevates two co-CEOs after founder Colin Higgins steps to executive chairman after 22 years.
Betterment hit with class action suit alleging undisclosed cash sweep profits from client deposits at partner banks with zero interest paid to clients.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: M&A heat in broking and specialty insurance, strong P&C fundamentals offset by AI/cyber regulatory headwinds.

bullets.text: KKR, Amwins, and Dragoneer bid A$7.7B ($5.3B) for Australian broker Steadfast, signaling continued appetite for consolidation in insurance distribution.
U.S. P&C industry posts strongest underwriting profit in a decade ($84B over two years) with lowest combined ratio in 10 years—pricing discipline is working.
KKR/Blue Owl-backed WoodStar Reciprocal Exchange launches with $220M+ in capital to provide capacity for Accelerant's specialty risk platform.
Canada's banking regulator warns institutions on cyber risks from advanced AI models like Anthropic's Claude, highlighting regulatory scrutiny on emerging tech adoption.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Five stories spanning payments infrastructure, private debt positioning, CMBS stress, fintech expansion, and regulatory headwinds.

bullets.text: Mastercard exploring Vocalink sale—UK payments platform acquired 2016 may return to original banking owners, signaling potential exit from legacy asset.
CMBS special servicing rates climbed in June 2026 on retail/office/lodging stress; watch for wave of servicer workouts and credit deterioration.
Nubank clears Mexican banking license despite fintech headwinds elsewhere; North America private debt still LP-favored despite market divergence.
Klarna hit with Dutch class action lawsuit—regulatory/litigation risk mounting for BNPL player in core European market.

PE & Deals.lede: GP financing innovation and secondaries expansion dominate—Arctos debuts novel structure while Moelis and majors consolidate.

bullets.text: Arctos Partners closed a $6.2B GP financing deal using a novel 'self-liquidating' structure, enabling capital returns to LPs while maintaining portfolio exposure—a first deployment of this approach in GP-financing markets.

Moelis hired a four-person team from Evercore to build out LP-led secondaries practice, tapping into a growth segment as LGT, Dawson, and Pomona capitalize on favorable pricing and liquidity conditions.
Apollo, Clearlake, and KKR are among 10 PE firms consolidating private markets businesses, reflecting broader industry push for scale in alternatives.

Markets & Macro.lede: Bank earnings season opens amid QE/QT structural risks; small caps rally on tech rotation; ECB digital euro pilots launch; UK gilt yields spike.

bullets.text: Bank earnings kick off Tuesday with market priced for high expectations—watch for potential disappointment and any signals on deposit stability post-QT.
QE fundamentally rewired bank balance sheets and funding models, making quantitative tightening riskier than central banks are accounting for—a structural headwind for the sector.
Russell 2000 on pace for best year since 2003 as capital rotates out of mega-cap tech into small-cap beneficiaries of AI spending and tax policy.
UK 10-year gilt yields breach 5% on oil surge and fiscal concerns just as new PM takes office; ECB enlists 36 banks (Deutsche, BPCE, DZ) to pilot digital euro in 2025.

Newsletters.lede: Big bank earnings dominate tomorrow's read on the economy; meanwhile, ETF redemptions spike, fintech workflows get smoother, and student loan policy reshapes advisory demand.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—credit quality, deposits, and consumer health will be front and center across lending, trading, and wealth management.
Rare week of negative ETF flows signals investor wariness; redemptions hitting equity, bond, and alternative products.
Vanguard and Envestnet launched tools to reduce tax friction on model portfolio conversions—removing a key pain point in RIA workflows.
Student loan policy overhaul is forcing advisors to rethink college planning strategies, reshaping demand for education financing products.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: 5 stories on incentives, AI moats, market competition theory, and Apple's strategic vulnerability—heavy on competitive dynamics.

bullets.text: Open-source AI now matches frontier models at 1/10th the cost, forcing founders to compete on moats beyond raw capability rather than model superiority.
Apple's OpenAI lawsuit over employee trade-secret theft masks the real problem: Apple lacks coherent AI strategy and is playing catch-up to competitors.
Tyler Cowen argues competitive markets exist only if P ≠ NP; if P = NP, firms can efficiently detect and sustain cartels, collapsing competition as equilibrium.

all.lede: Big week ahead: US bank earnings dominate sentiment, KKR leads $5.3B push for Australian broker Steadfast, and QT risks loom as structural bank vulnerabilities emerge.

bullets.text: KKR joins Amwins and Dragoneer in A$7.7B ($5.3B) bid for Steadfast, Australia's largest insurance broker—competitive process heating up in insurance M&A.
Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings Tuesday/Wednesday; results will signal credit quality, deposit trends, and consumer health across lending and wealth management.
QE structurally rewired bank balance sheets and funding models, making quantitative tightening riskier than policymakers assume due to banks' newfound dependence on central bank liquidity.
Mastercard exploring Vocalink sale—potentially returning UK payments platform to original bank owners after 2016 acquisition.
